Name: hardware_fusion_cache_optimization
Title: Optimizing Hardware-Level Kernel Fusion and Cache Utilization for Efficient Inference Throughput
Short Hypothesis: By strategically fusing CUDA kernels and optimizing cache layouts specifically for tensor operations, we can achieve significant reductions in latency and increases in throughput that surpass existing state-of-the-art techniques, addressing the hardware challenges in inference workloads.
Related Work: Existing literature focuses on theoretical frameworks for optimizing inference, such as the training-free geometric methods or spectral approaches. This proposal distinguishes itself by implementing low-level, empirical optimizations at the kernel level and cache architecture, targeting measurable performance improvements on commodity GPUs rather than theoretical bounds.
Abstract: This proposal aims to enhance machine learning inference throughput through hardware-level optimizations by focusing on kernel fusion and cache utilization. While current methods often emphasize theoretical constructs, we will provide a practical approach that leverages empirical profiling to identify bottlenecks in CUDA kernels and memory access patterns. Our methodology will involve the design of fused CUDA kernels specifically tailored for common tensor operations, ensuring optimal memory coalescence and reduced bandwidth usage. Furthermore, we will explore innovative cache layouts to enhance data locality and minimize latency. Through rigorous benchmarking on commodity GPUs, we will examine the effects of these optimizations on wall-clock latency and tokens-per-second throughput. This work not only aims to push the boundaries of inference performance but also to provide reproducible benchmarks that practitioners can adopt immediately, bridging the gap between theoretical advancements and practical deployment.
Experiments: Conduct kernel fusion experiments by combining commonly used tensor operations and measure the achieved latency and throughput using nvprof/Nsight.
Implement different cache layouts for tensor operations and evaluate their impact on memory bandwidth utilization and execution time.
Perform A/B testing under fixed accuracy budgets to compare the performance of optimized kernels against baseline implementations, recording p50/p99 latency and throughput metrics.
Test the effects of varying batch sizes on performance to assess efficiency in real-world deployment scenarios.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential overfitting to specific hardware configurations, limiting generalizability to other architectures.
Dependence on the accuracy of profiling tools to capture true performance metrics, which may vary across different workloads.
The complexity of implementing and maintaining optimized kernels could lead to increased development time and potential bugs.
